samples.text: If the seed is planted and watered, then a sprout appears. It is not the case that the seed is planted and watered. Does it follow that a sprout appears?
Whenever the seed is planted and watered, a sprout appears. However, in this situation it is not true that the seed is planted and watered. Can we conclude that a sprout appears?
If the seed is sown and irrigated, then a shoot emerges. It is not the case that the seed is sown and irrigated. Does it follow that a shoot emerges?
The only stated cause of 'a sprout appears' is 'the seed is planted and watered'. That cause did not occur. Can we conclude that a sprout appears?
If the seed is planted and watered, then a sprout appears. It is not the case that the seed is planted and watered. This topic was discussed last week. Does it follow that a sprout appears?
